raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.7,"R1.5":0.3,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.7,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.4","R1.6","R1.7","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"The open-ended request invites assessment of whether the thesis, gap, and contribution claim are framed persuasively.","R1.2":"A general reaction to the draft centrally includes checking whether the theoretical argument chain coheres.","R1.3":"The artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims that should be assessed against the reported results and bounds.","R1.4":"The text explicitly claims a first-of-its-kind guarantee, making novelty assessment central to the expected response.","R1.6":"An open-ended opinion on a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.","R1.7":"Because the artifact relies on named prior work and theorem attribution, citation coverage and accuracy are central responsibilities.","RX.1":"Any response should disclose uncertainty, especially because only an excerpt is available.","RX.2":"The evaluator must avoid endorsing broad novelty or performance claims beyond what the artifact supports.","RX.3":"The response should stay within giving an opinion on the paper excerpt rather than executing edits or external validation.","RX.4":"The projection should account for possible harm from overstating correctness of a numerical method or its guarantees.","RX.5":"Any assessment should preserve traceability to the specific claims, citations, and reported results in the artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: The open-ended request invites assessment of whether the thesis, gap, and contribution claim are framed persuasively.
rationale.R1.2: A general reaction to the draft centrally includes checking whether the theoretical argument chain coheres.
rationale.R1.3: The artifact makes empirical and theoretical claims that should be assessed against the reported results and bounds.
rationale.R1.4: The text explicitly claims a first-of-its-kind guarantee, making novelty assessment central to the expected response.
rationale.R1.6: An open-ended opinion on a paper excerpt reasonably includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, and register.
rationale.R1.7: Because the artifact relies on named prior work and theorem attribution, citation coverage and accuracy are central responsibilities.
rationale.RX.1: Any response should disclose uncertainty, especially because only an excerpt is available.
rationale.RX.2: The evaluator must avoid endorsing broad novelty or performance claims beyond what the artifact supports.
rationale.RX.3: The response should stay within giving an opinion on the paper excerpt rather than executing edits or external validation.
rationale.RX.4: The projection should account for possible harm from overstating correctness of a numerical method or its guarantees.
rationale.RX.5: Any assessment should preserve traceability to the specific claims, citations, and reported results in the artifact.
